title: Cybersecurity Stocks Are Holding Up as the AI Trade Starts to Crack
excerpt: As semiconductors and AI trade leaders sell off, cybersecurity stocks like CrowdStrike, Fortinet, and Palo Alto Networks hold near highs, hinting at a market...
content_html:
Ryan Hasson, MarketBeat

Mon, July 20, 2026 at 8:05 AM CDT 4 min read

Key Points

  • Interested in CrowdStrike? Here are five stocks we like better.

  • Cybersecurity stocks have shown notable relative strength as many semiconductor and AI infrastructure names have pulled back.

  • CrowdStrike, Fortinet and Palo Alto Networks remain near their highs, supported by AI-driven security demand and platform strategies.

  • Valuations and analyst price targets remain key risks, but the group's resilience could signal a leadership rotation.

The AI trade, at least for the time being, has come to an end. Many leaders have officially broken below major support and key moving averages, signaling a change in market momentum. Semiconductors, memory names, neoclouds, photonics, the groups that led the market for most of the year, have sold off hard over the past month. But one corner of technology has barely flinched: cybersecurity. And in a tape like this, that kind of relative strength is exactly what tends to precede leadership changes.

The numbers tell the story cleanly. The Amplify Cybersecurity ETF (NYSEARCA: HACK) is up 15.4% over the past 30 days and hit a fresh 12-month high in early July. Over that same 30-day stretch, the VanEck Semiconductor ETF (NASDAQ: SMH) fell almost 9%. The gap widens further when measured against their peaks. HACK sits less than 5% below its 52-week high, while SMH trades roughly 17% below its own, and many memory and semiconductor leaders sit 15% to 25% off their recent highs. It is worth noting that Bank of America just called long semiconductors the most crowded trade ever, while cybersecurity stocks actually rose on one of the market's ugliest sessions last week.

→ Buyback Boom: These 3 Companies Are Betting Billions on Their Own Stocks

When money rotates out of a crowded theme, it looks for quality groups with their own demand drivers. Cybersecurity, with rising AI-driven threats and non-discretionary budgets, fits that description well. Three names in particular have been leading the way.

CrowdStrike: The Platform Leader Keeps Compounding

CrowdStrike (NASDAQ: CRWD) is up around 75% year to date and trades within striking distance of its 52-week high of $217.50. That momentum and outperformance have been backed by real business progress.

→ 3 Photonics Companies Making Quantum Tech Possible

The company recently expanded its strategic partnership with Schwarz Digits to deliver sovereign cybersecurity across Europe and agreed to acquire XM Cyber's IP, while Frost & Sullivan named it Company of the Year for identity threat detection.

content_html_zh:
瑞安·哈森，MarketBeat

2026 年 7 月 20 日星期一上午 8:05 CDT 阅读时间 4 分钟

要点

  • 对 CrowdStrike 感兴趣？ 这是我们更喜欢的五只股票。
  • 随着许多半导体和人工智能基础设施公司纷纷撤资，网络安全股票表现出显着的相对强势。
  • 在人工智能驱动的安全需求和平台战略的支持下，CrowdStrike、Fortinet 和 Palo Alto Networks 仍保持在高位附近。
  • 估值和分析师价格目标仍然是主要风险，但该集团的韧性可能预示着领导层的轮换。

人工智能贸易至少目前已经结束。 许多领先者已正式跌破主要支撑位和关键移动平均线，表明市场势头发生变化。 半导体、存储器、新云、光子学等今年大部分时间引领市场的板块在过去一个月遭遇大幅抛售。 但技术的一个角落几乎没有退缩：网络安全。 在这样的磁带中，这种相对优势恰恰是领导层变动之前的趋势。

数字清晰地讲述了故事。 Amplify Cyber​​security ETF（NYSEARCA：HACK）在过去 30 天里上涨了 15.4%，并在 7 月初创下了 12 个月新高。 在同一 30 天的时间里，VanEck Semiconductor ETF（纳斯达克股票代码：SMH）下跌了近 9%。 与峰值相比，差距进一步扩大。 HACK 的股价较 52 周高点下跌不到 5%，而 SMH 的股价较其近期高点下跌约 17%，许多内存和半导体领军企业的股价较近期高点下跌 15% 至 25%。 值得注意的是，美国银行刚刚称多头半导体是有史以来最拥挤的交易，而网络安全股票实际上在上周市场最丑陋的交易之一中上涨。

→ 回购热潮：这 3 家公司在自己的股票上押注数十亿美元

当资金从拥挤的主题中流出时，它会寻找具有自己的需求驱动力的优质群体。 网络安全，随着人工智能驱动的威胁的增加和不可自由支配的预算，非常符合这一描述。 尤其是三个名字一直处于领先地位。

CrowdStrike：平台领导者不断复合

CrowdStrike（纳斯达克股票代码：CRWD）今年迄今已上涨约 75%，交易价格接近 52 周高点 217.50 美元。 这种势头和优异的业绩得到了实际业务进展的支持。

→ 3 家光子学公司使量子技术成为可能

该公司最近扩大了与 Schwarz Digits 的战略合作伙伴关系，以在整个欧洲提供主权网络安全，并同意收购 XM Cyber​​ 的 IP，而 Frost & Sullivan 则将其评为身份威胁检测年度最佳公司。
